channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Supreme Court rules that prison guards can't be sued for shaving Rastafarian's head
item.description: The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court ruled that a Louisiana prisoner whose dreadlocks were forcibly shaved off by prison guards cannot sue the guards under a federal law to protect the religious rights of prisoners.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg

item.title: Meet the creator of the Story Spine, an 8-sentence tool to create and analyze stories
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks to playwright Kenn Adams about his formula for creating or analyzing stories called the Story Spine.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks to playwright Kenn Adams about his formula for creating or analyzing stories called the Story Spine.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: 'The Adventures of Juan Planchard': A political thriller that reads like a blockbuster
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with author and filmmaker Jonathan Jakubowicz about his book The Adventures of Juan Planchard, now translated into English.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with author and filmmaker Jonathan Jakubowicz about his book The Adventures of Juan Planchard, now translated into English.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Marquez Janse

item.title: In one of the U.S.'s toss-up Congressional districts, cost of living is a top concern
item.description: The New York district represented by Republican Rep. Mike Lawler is a toss-up and one Democrats are eyeing to pick up in their quest for control of Congress.
item.content:encoded: The New York district represented by Republican Rep. Mike Lawler is a toss-up and one Democrats are eyeing to pick up in their quest for control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Steve Kastenbaum

item.title: Boaters compete in 750-mile race... with no engines
item.description: For the "Race to Alaska," intrepid boaters compete in a 750-mile no-engine boat race from Washington state to Alaska.
item.content:encoded: For the "Race to Alaska," intrepid boaters compete in a 750-mile no-engine boat race from Washington state to Alaska.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Morrison

item.title: A musical wedding tradition puts Philly grooms to the test
item.description: Philadelphia is home to a unique wedding custom you've probably never heard of.
item.content:encoded: Philadelphia is home to a unique wedding custom you've probably never heard of.
item.dc:creator: Justin Kramon

item.title: Senate passes war powers resolution, Tim Kaine says there's more work to be done
item.description: The Senate has voted to take away President Trump's power to wage war with Iran. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Virginia Democrat Tim Kaine, a sponsor of the bill.
item.content:encoded: The Senate has voted to take away President Trump's power to wage war with Iran. NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Virginia Democrat Tim Kaine, a sponsor of the bill.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Marquez Janse

item.title: Why Trump (and other politicians) are going to sporting events
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with University of Richmond political scientist Dan Palazzolo about the politics of officials affiliating with sports.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with University of Richmond political scientist Dan Palazzolo about the politics of officials affiliating with sports.
item.dc:creator: Megan Lim

item.title: Efforts to protect gorillas from Ebola in the DRC
item.description: The staff of a wildlife preserve in the Democratic Republic of Congo are trying desperately to keep their gorillas from exposure to Ebola. Gorillas can get it and almost all of them die from it.
item.content:encoded: The staff of a wildlife preserve in the Democratic Republic of Congo are trying desperately to keep their gorillas from exposure to Ebola. Gorillas can get it and almost all of them die from it.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Conflict is escalating between President Trump and Senate Republicans
item.description: Ties between President Trump and Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., are being tested, as some Senate Republicans say the president's unpredictable actions are derailing their shared agenda.
item.content:encoded: Ties between President Trump and Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., are being tested, as some Senate Republicans say the president's unpredictable actions are derailing their shared agenda.
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas
